metrics.narrative: Pain is well documented but not converted into a quantified business case. On 2026-03-10 the buyer named 'an urgent need for an iFAX module and a supply information module, along with significant improvements in performance and dashboards, which have become increasingly difficult to manage.' On 2026-06-03 the buyer framed the initiative as a multi-year, multi-phase digital transformation ('S&C is so far behind on the digital transformation'). No dollar-figure ROI, cycle-time, or savings target has been voiced by the buyer on any read call.

sources: Avoma notes, Quick SRM Touchbase, 2026-06-03
champion.narrative: Katie Booth (Global Indirect Sourcing Manager, IT) is the most consistently engaged buyer contact across nearly every touchpoint from January through June 2026, drove demo scoring/scheduling logistics, and per Next_Step__c a 2026-06-24 meeting with her was held specifically 'to confirm Zycus is vendor of choice going into final demo.' Yufeng Chen has also acted as an internal advocate, defending required features against IT-driven scope cuts (2026-06-03: 'Business stakeholders (Kara and Yufeng) will actively defend required features and not accept IT-driven scope cuts'). Neither has demonstrated direct access to the Economic Buyer (Jim Boss) or CIO (Bob King) in any read call, and Katie Booth's own title sits inside IT sourcing, the same organization whose CIO is currently pushing Oracle, so her internal leverage against that specific headwind is unproven.

sources: Avoma transcript, S&C - Zycus Demo connect, 2026-05-14
competition.narrative: A clean three-way field: Zycus, Oracle, and iValua. Next_Step__c states 'iValua in only because they were shortlisted last initiative and Oracle at IT's insistence,' and on 2026-05-14 a buyer speaker asked Zycus directly, 'I know you can't say it, but is Oracle one of them because it's your ERP?' The buyer's own team has voiced discomfort with Oracle as a partner even while IT insists on including it, per Next_Step__c ('no one is happy with Oracle as a partner'), giving Zycus a real but politically contested edge.

economic_buyer.narrative: Salesforce contact roles tag Jim Boss (VP Global Operational Excellence) as both Decision Maker and Executive Sponsor, which under the SF-override rule counts as identified. Next_Step__c (2026-07-02) independently references 'the VP of Supply Chain - the executive Sponsor wants a vendor-led implementation,' consistent with either Jim Boss or Kara Weiner, who was named in the 2026-01-14 call as the new VP of Supply Chain driving the initiative after a reorganization. However, neither Jim Boss, Kara Weiner, nor David Single (VP Finance, tagged Evaluator/Influencer) has appeared as a speaker on any of the 12 deep-read Avoma calls; all EB signal is inferred through the sourcing/procurement team, not direct EB-to-Zycus contact.

decision_process.narrative: Process is structured: ~35 SME evaluators score each module against the SOW, a demo evaluation window was set for 2026-06-22 to 2026-06-30 (Avoma, 2026-06-08), and an on-site executive review of demo outcomes was planned for early July (Avoma, 2026-06-08). By 2026-06-30 the actual demo dates had slipped to the week of 2026-07-08 to 07-10 or 07-13 to 07-17, and an IT deep-dive with the buyer's IT lead ('Pete') on integration scoping and the Oracle comparison remains pending per Next_Step__c. The process is real and detailed but has slipped against its own internal schedule.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are unusually explicit: a 217-line RFP/SOW with each requirement numbered and tagged (e.g. RFX01, eProc03) and classified as out-of-box, configurable, custom, or custom-build (Avoma, 2026-05-14). A common scorecard evaluation matrix covers UX, change management, AI strategy, supplier support and total cost (Avoma, 2026-01-30), and the demo scoring sheet mirrors the SOW's own tab order and priority so evaluators score consistently (Avoma, 2026-06-30).

critical_signals.text: The buyer's own team confirmed Zycus as vendor of choice heading into the final demo, but the IT organization is separately insisting Oracle stay in the running purely because it is the incumbent ERP, and the buyer team itself admits no one is happy with Oracle as a partner.

Against the buyer's 217-line requirement set, Zycus is tracking to meet roughly 80-85% out of the box, with 5-10% (mostly direct-materials PLM and inventory integration) not buildable at all without new development.

The buyer has asked repeatedly for a formal, dollar-figure RFP response so leadership can evaluate budget, and as of the latest evidence Zycus had not yet delivered it, even as the buyer's own internal deadline for the final RFP submission sits just six days before the deal's close date.

In the most recent fully-read call, Zycus committed to building the direct-materials PLM and inventory-visibility gaps through a custom application effort, and the buyer agreed to keep those items open as line items rather than treat them as disqualifying.

north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Push to Best Case with a realistic close in September 2026, allowing time after the 18 Jul RFP submission for demo scoring, executive review, pricing sign-off, and a paper process that has not yet begun.

flags: No quantified business case: qualitative pain (vendor-led implementation need, change-management constraint) is documented but no ROI, cost savings, cycle-time reduction, or headcount impact. Economic Buyer and Executive Sponsor have not articulated financial metrics. This weakens forecast defensibility and leaves the deal vulnerable if budget or priority shifts.

Paper-process timeline gap: 18 days to close, no documented MSA/SOW milestones, legal-review process, or InfoSec engagement despite Harman Toor (Corporate Counsel) in contact roles. Risk of contracting delay if not surfaced and planned in final demo conversation.

Economic buyer passive: Jim Boss (Executive Sponsor, four contact roles) and David Single (VP Finance, three contact roles) are mapped but have not engaged on pricing, ROI, or budget sign-off across 14 calls. The move: escalate to the deal owner's manager (Alexa Bradley) for an executive connect to the deal owner's manager or David Single to secure budget approval and ROI sign-off before contracting. Without EB active sponsorship, forecast stays Best Case and contracting timeline is at risk.

Convert the verbal 'vendor of choice' signal into a written scoring understanding before demos start, protecting Zycus from being penalized on the 15-20% direct-materials deviation in the 217-line RFP.
